body:
This is a large refactor and simplification of the Meshes and Topologies modules:

- Meshes are now all very lightweight objects, currently supporting interval, rectangles and cubed spheres
  -  A `RectangleMesh` is now explicitly a product of two `IntervalMesh`s
  - `RectangleMesh` replaces both `EquispacedRectangleMesh` and `TensorProductMesh`
  - There are various `AbstractCubedSphereMesh`es, which now encode the warping functions
- Meshes now have a relatively well-specified API: basically the key function is `opposing_face` (all other properties are computed from this).
   - It internally now uses Base Julia `CartesianIndex` machinery, which gets rid of a lot of manual indexing we had to do previously.
   - Cubed spheres now use a different panel ordering (it matches the S2Geometry library and Oceananigans).
   - Provide convenience functions for computing face and vertex connectivity matrices.
- There is now only 1 2D Topology object, called (unimaginatively) `Topology2D`. This is a heavier object, in that it stores all the interior face pairs, unique vertices and boundary faces, but is the same for all meshes.
  - It has an option for different element orderings (e.g. to use space-filling curves)
  - This will allow adding a new `DistributedTopology2D` type (#323), which should be able to use the same mesh interface.
title: Refactor mesh and topology code
